seed_problem: Place P depots (fixed across K seasons) among N city addresses with seasonal demand; score the worst-season weighted nearest-depot assignment cost.
mutation: composite: compose demand-geometry-clustering + facility-location + scenario-minimax. Depots are sited ONCE but graded on the MAXIMUM over K seasonal demand vectors of the weighted nearest-depot cost. The generator plants geographically disjoint per-season hot zones with EQUAL mass but UNEQUAL spread, so the pooled/average k-means recipe chases density and starves the spread zones, whose season becomes the worst; the insightful layout clusters against the worst-case (max-over-seasons) envelope and hedges depots into the spread zones.
theme: courier depots that survive every season
title: Courier Depots That Survive Every Season
strategies: single-hub baseline: all P depots at the unweighted centroid (matches the checker baseline, ratio ~0.1)
pooled k-means (the trap): weighted Lloyd on the summed-over-seasons demand using L2 means; packs depots onto the compact/dense zones and starves the spread zones, so the worst season blows up

envelope minimax hedge (the insight): initialise on max-over-seasons demand, use L1 coordinate-wise weighted medians, and iteratively upweight the currently-worst season so extra depots migrate into the spread zones that set F
local-search reallocation: from any layout, move the depot cluster that helps the worst season most, re-evaluating the max each step
